#[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n57\t    error: Option<Value>,\n58\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n59\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n60\t    error_type: Option<String>,\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\t/// Response for write operations.\n64\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n65\tpub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n66\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n67\t    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n68\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n69\t    indexUid: Option<String>,\n70\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n71\t    status: Option<String>,\n72\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n73\t    error: Option<String>,\n74\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n75\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n76\t    error_type: Option<String>,\n77\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n78\t    code: Option<String>,\n79\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n80\t    link: Option<String>,\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\t/// Header name for degraded write responses.\n84\tpub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n85\t\n86\t/// Header name for idempotency key.\n87\tpub const HEADER_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY: &str = \"Idempotency-Key\";\n88\t\n89\t/// Compute SHA256 hash of request body for idempotency check.\n90\tfn compute_body_hash(documents: &Value) -> String {\n91\t    let canonical = if let Some(arr) = documents.as_array() {\n92\t        // Sort array elements by primary key for deterministic hash\n93\t        let mut sorted: Vec<_> = arr.iter().collect();\n94\t        sorted.sort_by_key(|doc| {\n95\t            doc.get(\"id\")\n96\t                .or_else(|| doc.get(\"pk\"))\n97\t                .or_else(|| doc.get(\"key\"))\n98\t                .or_else(|| doc.get(\"_id\"))\n99\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n100\t                .unwrap_or(\"\")\n101\t        });\n102\t        serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_default()\n103\t    } else {\n104\t        serde_json::to_string(documents).unwrap_or_default()\n105\t    };\n106\t\n107\t    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n108\t    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n109\t    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t/// Extract idempotency key from headers.\n113\t/// Returns None if header is missing or invalid.\n114\tfn extract_idempotency_key(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n115\t    headers\n116\t        .get(HEADER_IDEMPOTENCY_KEY)\n117\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n118\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n119\t        .map(|s| s.to_string())\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\t/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n124\tstruct QuorumState {\n125\t    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n126\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n127\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n128\t    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n129\t    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n130\t    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n131\t    /// Total degraded groups count\n132\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\timpl QuorumState {\n136\t    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n137\t    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n138\t        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n139\t    }\n140\t\n141\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n142\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n143\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n144\t    }\n145\t\n146\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n147\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n148\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n149\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n153\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n154\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n155\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n156\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n157\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n158\t        met\n159\t    }\n160\t\n161\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n162\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n163\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n167\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n168\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n169\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n170\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n171\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n172\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n173\t                    degraded += 1;\n174\t                }\n175\t            }\n176\t        }\n177\t        degraded\n178\t    }\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n182\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n183\twhere\n184\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n185\t{\n186\t    Router::new()\n187\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n188\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n189\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n190\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n191\t}\n192\t\n193\t/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n194\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n195\tasync fn post_documents(\n196\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n197\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n198\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n199\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n200\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n201\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n202\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n203\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n204\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n205\t        let s = ext.0;\n206\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n207\t    });\n208\t\n209\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, Some(headers)).await\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n213\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n214\tasync fn put_documents(\n215\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n216\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n217\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n218\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n219\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n220\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n221\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n222\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n223\t        let s = ext.0;\n224\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n225\t    });\n226\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, Some(headers)).await\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n230\tasync fn delete_documents(\n231\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n232\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n233\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n234\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n235\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n236\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n237\t        let s = ext.0;\n238\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n239\t    });\n240\t\n241\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n242\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n243\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n244\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n245\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n246\t            origin: None, // Client write\n247\t        };\n248\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n249\t    }\n250\t\n251\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n252\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n253\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n254\t            .iter()\n255\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n256\t            .collect();\n257\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n258\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n259\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n260\t                ids,\n261\t                origin: None, // Client write\n262\t            };\n263\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n268\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n269\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n270\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n271\t    ))\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n275\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n276\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n277\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n278\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n279\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n280\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n281\t        let s = ext.0;\n282\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n283\t    });\n284\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n285\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n286\t        ids: vec![id],\n287\t        origin: None, // Client write\n288\t    };\n289\t    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n293\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n294\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n295\t    index: String,\n296\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n297\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n298\t    state: &AppState,\n299\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n300\t    headers: Option<HeaderMap>,\n301\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n302\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n303\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n304\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n305\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n306\t        ));\n307\t    }\n308\t\n309\t    // Check idempotency if enabled (plan §13.10)\n310\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n311\t        if let Some(ref headers) = headers {\n312\t            if let Some(idempotency_key) = extract_idempotency_key(headers) {\n313\t                let body_hash = compute_body_hash(&serde_json::to_value(&documents).unwrap_or(Value::Null));\n314\t\n315\t                // Check cache for existing entry\n316\t                match state.idempotency_cache.check(&idempotency_key, &body_hash).await {\n317\t                    Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n318\t                        // Idempotency hit - return cached mtask ID\n319\t                        state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n320\t                        debug!(\n321\t                            idempotency_key = %idempotency_key,\n322\t                            cached_mtask_id = %cached_mtask_id,\n323\t                            \"idempotency hit - returning cached mtask ID\"\n324\t                        );\n325\t                        return Ok(build_response_with_degraded_header(\n326\t                            DocumentsWriteResponse {\n327\t                                taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n328\t                                indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n329\t                                status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n330\t                                error: None,\n331\t                                error_type: None,\n332\t                                code: None,\n333\t                                link: None,\n334\t                            },\n335\t                            0, // No degradation on cached response\n336\t                        )?);\n337\t                    }\n338\t                    Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n339\t                        // Same key, different body - return 409 Conflict\n340\t                        state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n341\t                        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n342\t                            MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n343\t                            \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n344\t                        ));\n345\t                    }\n346\t                    Ok(None) => {\n347\t                        // Cache miss - proceed with write\n348\t                        state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n349\t                        debug!(\n350\t                            idempotency_key = %idempotency_key,\n351\t                            \"idempotency miss - proceeding with write\"\n352\t                        );\n353\t                    }\n354\t                    Err(_) => {\n355\t                        // Other error - proceed with write (fail-open)\n356\t                        state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n357\t                    }\n358\t                }\n359\t            }\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n364\t    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n365\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n366\t        // Check if the index is an alias\n367\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n368\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n369\t            // It was an alias\n370\t            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n371\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n372\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n373\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n374\t                    format!(\n375\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n376\t                        index\n377\t                    ),\n378\t                ));\n379\t            }\n380\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n381\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n382\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n383\t        } else {\n384\t            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n385\t            index.clone()\n386\t        }\n387\t    } else {\n388\t        index.clone()\n389\t    };\n390\t\n391\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n392\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n393\t        documents\n394\t            .first()\n395\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n396\t    });\n397\t\n398\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n399\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n400\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n401\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n402\t        )\n403\t    })?;\n404\t\n405\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n406\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n407\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n408\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n409\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n410\t\n411\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n412\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n413\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n414\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n415\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n416\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n417\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n418\t            ));\n419\t        }\n420\t\n421\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n422\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n423\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n424\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n425\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n426\t            ));\n427\t        }\n428\t\n429\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n430\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n431\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n432\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n433\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n434\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n435\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n436\t            ));\n437\t        }\n438\t\n439\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n440\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n441\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n442\t                format!(\n443\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n444\t                    i, primary_key\n445\t                ),\n446\t            ));\n447\t        }\n448\t    }\n449\t\n450\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n451\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n452\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n453\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n454\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n455\t\n456\t    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n457\t    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n458\t        Some(\n459\t            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n460\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n461\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n462\t                .as_millis() as u64\n463\t        )\n464\t    } else {\n465\t        None\n466\t    };\n467\t\n468\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n469\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n470\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n471\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n472\t        }\n473\t\n474\t        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n475\t        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n476\t        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n477\t            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n478\t        }\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n482\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n483\t\n484\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n485\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n486\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n487\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n488\t    );\n489\t\n490\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n491\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n492\t\n493\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n494\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n495\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n496\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n497\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n498\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n499\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n500\t                    c.read().await\n501\t                })\n502\t            })\n503\t        });\n504\t\n505\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n506\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n507\t            shard_id,\n508\t            &topology,\n509\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n510\t        );\n511\t\n512\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n513\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n514\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n515\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n516\t            ));\n517\t        }\n518\t\n519\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n520\t\n521\t        for node_id in targets {\n522\t            let node = topology\n523\t                .node(&node_id)\n524\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n525\t\n526\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n527\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n528\t\n529\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n530\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n531\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n532\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n533\t                origin: None, // Client write\n534\t            };\n535\t\n536\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n537\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n538\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n539\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n540\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n541\t                    }\n542\t                }\n543\t                Ok(resp) => {\n544\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n545\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n546\t                }\n547\t                Err(_) => {\n548\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n549\t                }\n550\t            }\n551\t        }\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n555\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n556\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n557\t\n558\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n559\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n560\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n561\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n562\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n563\t        ));\n564\t    }\n565\t\n566\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n567\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n568\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n569\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n570\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n571\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n572\t            acks >= quorum\n573\t        })\n574\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n575\t\n576\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n577\t    let miroir_task = state\n578\t        .task_registry\n579\t        .register_with_metadata(\n580\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n581\t            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n582\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n583\t        )\n584\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n585\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n586\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n587\t        ))?;\n588\t\n589\t    // 7.2. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n590\t    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n591\t        if let Some(ref headers) = headers {\n592\t            if let Some(idempotency_key) = extract_idempotency_key(headers) {\n593\t                let body_hash = compute_body_hash(&serde_json::to_value(&documents).unwrap_or(Value::Null));\n594\t                state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n595\t                    idempotency_key,\n596\t                    body_hash,\n597\t                    miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n598\t                ).await;\n599\t                debug!(\n600\t                    mtask_id = %miroir_task.miroir_id,\n601\t                    \"inserted idempotency cache entry\"\n602\t                );\n603\t            }\n604\t        }\n605\t    }\n606\t\n607\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n608\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n609\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n610\t            sid,\n611\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n612\t            first_quorum_group,\n613\t        ).await {\n614\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n615\t            tracing::error!(\n616\t                session_id = %sid,\n617\t                error = %e,\n618\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n619\t            );\n620\t        }\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n624\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n625\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n626\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n627\t            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n628\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n629\t            error: None,\n630\t            error_type: None,\n631\t            code: None,\n632\t            link: None,\n633\t        },\n634\t        degraded_groups,\n635\t    )\n636\t}\n637\t\n638\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n639\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n640\t    index: String,\n641\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n642\t    state: &AppState,\n643\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n644\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n645\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n646\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n647\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n648\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n649\t        ));\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n653\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n654\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n655\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n656\t            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n657\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n658\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n659\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n660\t                    format!(\n661\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n662\t                        index\n663\t                    ),\n664\t                ));\n665\t            }\n666\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n667\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n668\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n669\t        } else {\n670\t            index.clone()\n671\t        }\n672\t    } else {\n673\t        index.clone()\n674\t    };\n675\t\n676\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n677\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n678\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n679\t\n680\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n681\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n682\t    for id in &req.ids {\n683\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n684\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n685\t    }\n686\t\n687\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n688\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n689\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n690\t    );\n691\t\n692\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n693\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n694\t\n695\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n696\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n697\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n698\t\n699\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n700\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n701\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n702\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n703\t            ));\n704\t        }\n705\t\n706\t        for node_id in targets {\n707\t            let node = topology\n708\t                .node(&node_id)\n709\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n710\t\n711\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n712\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n713\t\n714\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n715\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n716\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n717\t                origin: None, // Client write\n718\t            };\n719\t\n720\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n721\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n722\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n723\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n724\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n725\t                    }\n726\t                }\n727\t                Ok(resp) => {\n728\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n729\t                }\n730\t                Err(_) => {\n731\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n732\t                }\n733\t            }\n734\t        }\n735\t    }\n736\t\n737\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n738\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n739\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n740\t\n741\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n742\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n743\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n744\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n745\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n746\t        ));\n747\t    }\n748\t\n749\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n750\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n751\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n752\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n753\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n754\t            acks >= quorum\n755\t        })\n756\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n757\t\n758\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n759\t    let miroir_task = state\n760\t        .task_registry\n761\t        .register_with_metadata(\n762\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n763\t            Some(index.clone()),\n764\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n765\t        )\n766\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n767\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n768\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n769\t        ))?;\n770\t\n771\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n772\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n773\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n774\t            sid,\n775\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n776\t            first_quorum_group,\n777\t        ).await {\n778\t            tracing::error!(\n779\t                session_id = %sid,\n780\t                error = %e,\n781\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n782\t            );\n783\t        }\n784\t    }\n785\t\n786\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n787\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n788\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n789\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n790\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n791\t            error: None,\n792\t            error_type: None,\n793\t            code: None,\n794\t            link: None,\n795\t        },\n796\t        degraded_groups,\n797\t    )\n798\t}\n799\t\n800\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n801\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n802\t    index: String,\n803\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n804\t    state: &AppState,\n805\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n806\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n807\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n808\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n809\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n810\t\n811\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n812\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n813\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n814\t    );\n815\t\n816\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n817\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n818\t\n819\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n820\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n821\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n822\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n823\t\n824\t        match client\n825\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n826\t            .await\n827\t        {\n828\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n829\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n830\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n831\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n832\t                }\n833\t            }\n834\t            Ok(resp) => {\n835\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n836\t            }\n837\t            Err(_) => {\n838\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n839\t            }\n840\t        }\n841\t    }\n842\t\n843\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n844\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n845\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n846\t\n847\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n848\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n849\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n850\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n851\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n852\t        ));\n853\t    }\n854\t\n855\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n856\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n857\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n858\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n859\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n860\t            acks >= quorum\n861\t        })\n862\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n863\t\n864\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n865\t    let miroir_task = state\n866\t        .task_registry\n867\t        .register_with_metadata(\n868\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n869\t            Some(index.clone()),\n870\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n871\t        )\n872\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n873\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n874\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n875\t        ))?;\n876\t\n877\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n878\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n879\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n880\t            sid,\n881\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n882\t            first_quorum_group,\n883\t        ).await {\n884\t            tracing::error!(\n885\t                session_id = %sid,\n886\t                error = %e,\n887\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n888\t            );\n889\t        }\n890\t    }\n891\t\n892\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n893\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n894\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n895\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n896\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n897\t            error: None,\n898\t            error_type: None,\n899\t            code: None,\n900\t            link: None,\n901\t        },\n902\t        degraded_groups,\n903\t    )\n904\t}\n905\t\n906\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n907\t///\n908\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n909\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n910\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n911\t        .iter()\n912\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n913\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n914\t}\n915\t\n916\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n917\t///\n918\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n919\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n920\t///\n921\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n922\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n923\t    documents: &[Value],\n924\t    primary_key: &str,\n925\t    topology: &Topology,\n926\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n927\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n928\t\n929\t    for doc in documents {\n930\t        let pk_value = doc\n931\t            .get(primary_key)\n932\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n933\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n934\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n935\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n936\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n937\t                )\n938\t            })?;\n939\t\n940\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n941\t        shard_documents\n942\t            .entry(shard_id)\n943\t            .or_default()\n944\t            .push(doc.clone());\n945\t    }\n946\t\n947\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n948\t}\n949\t\n950\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n951\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n952\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n953\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n954\t        indexUid: None,\n955\t        status: None,\n956\t        error: resp.message,\n957\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n958\t        code: resp.code,\n959\t        link: None,\n960\t    }\n961\t}\n962\t\n963\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n964\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n965\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n966\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n967\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n968\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n969\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n970\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n971\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n972\t        )\n973\t    })?;\n974\t\n975\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n976\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n977\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n978\t\n979\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n980\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n981\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n982\t    }\n983\t\n984\t    Ok(builder\n985\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n986\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n987\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n988\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n989\t        ))?)\n990\t}\n991\t\n992\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n993\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n994\t    (\n995\t        StatusCode::OK,\n996\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n997\t        Json(resp),\n998\t    )\n999\t        .into_response()\n1000\t}\n1001\t\n1002\t#[cfg(test)]\n1003\tmod tests {\n1004\t    use super::*;\n1005\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n1006\t    use serde_json::json;\n1007\t\n1008\t    #[test]\n1009\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n1010\t        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1011\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n1012\t\n1013\t        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1014\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n1015\t\n1016\t        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1017\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n1018\t\n1019\t        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n1020\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n1021\t    }\n1022\t\n1023\t    #[test]\n1024\t    fn }\n1028\t\n1029\t    #[test]\n1030\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n1031\t        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n1032\t        let doc = `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n1041\t    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n1042\t\n1043\t    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n1044\t    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n1045\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n1046\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n1047\t            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n1048\t        )\n1049\t    }\n1050\t\n1051\t    #[test]\n1052\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n1053\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n1054\t        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n1055\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n1056\t    #[test]\n1073\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n1074\t        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n1075\t        let field = #[test]\n1085\t    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n1086\t        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n1087\t        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": }\n1091\t\n1092\t    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n1093\t    ///\n1094\t    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n1095\t    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n1096\t    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n1097\t    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n1098\t    /// | Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\n172\tpub fn count_assignment_diff(\n173\t    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n174\t    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n175\t) -> usize {\n176\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n177\t        .iter()\n178\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n179\t        .collect();\n180\t\n181\t    let mut diff = 0;\n182\t    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n183\t        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n184\t            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t    diff\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t#[cfg(test)]\n196\tmod tests {\n197\t    use super::*;\n198\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n199\t    use std::collections::HashMap;\n200\t\n201\t    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n202\t    #[test]\n203\t    fn test_determinism() {\n204\t        let nodes =  fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n420\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n421\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n422\t\n423\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n424\t        let P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n163\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n164\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n165\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, {\n176\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n177\t        .iter()\n178\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n179\t        .collect();\n180\t\n181\t    let change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t  fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n420\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n421\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n422\t\n423\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n424\t        let    }\n534\t    }\n535\t\n536\t    /// P1.3-A4: covering_set size is bounded by Ng (nodes in group).\n537\t    #[test]\n538\t    fn test_covering_set_size_bound() {\n539\t        let mut topo = outputs.\n561\t    #[test]\n562\t    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n563\t        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n564\t        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n565\t\n566\t        for i in 0u32..6 {\n567\t            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n568\t            let node = Node::new(\n569\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n570\t std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n585\t            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n586\t            assert_eq!(\n587\t                s1, P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n631\t    #[test]\n632\t    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n633\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n634\t        for i in 0u32..4 {\n635\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n636\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n637\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n638\t                0,\n639\t            ));\n640\t        }\n641\t\n642\t        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n643\t        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n644\t        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n645\t        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n646\t\n647\t        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n648\t        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n649\t            let replicas = assert!(c0.contains(r0), \"query_seq=0 should select first replica\");\n655\t            assert!(c1.contains(r1), \"query_seq=1 should select second replica\");\n656\t            assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n657\t        }\n658\t    }\n659\t}\n660\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b859d9cc-467d-4478-a522-3d8ed5268b70","uuid":"92da53d7-aeb6-4d60-8d8f-310895f67d25","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:40:49.172Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n/// for deterministic resolution.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n///\n/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\npub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n    shard_id: u32,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n\n    // Start with standard write targets\n    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n\n    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n\n                    // Only add if not already in targets\n                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    targets\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n///\n/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    if replica_groups == 0 {\n        return 0;\n    }\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n    shard_count: u32,\n    group: &Group,\n    rf: usize,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n    let mut result = Vec::new();\n    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            // No eligible replica for this shard\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n            result.push(selected.clone());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Some(result)\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n///\n/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\npub fn count_assignment_diff(\n    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n) -> usize {\n    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut diff = 0;\n    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n            for node in new_nodes {\n                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n                    diff += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    diff\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_determinism() {\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let reference = (0..100)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        // Run 1000 times and compare to reference\n        for _ in 0..1000 {\n            let current = (0..100)\n                .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n            assert_eq!(reference, current, \"Assignment is non-deterministic\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test 2: Reshuffle bound on add — 64 shards, 3→4 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 shard-node edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"Add reshuffle exceeded bound: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 3: Reshuffle bound on remove — 64 shards, 4→3 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: ~RF × S / Ng edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_4 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        // Expected ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32\n        let expected_diff = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n\n        assert!(\n            (diff as isize - expected_diff as isize).abs() <= expected_diff as isize / 2,\n            \"Remove reshuffle deviated significantly from expected: {} vs ~{}\",\n            diff,\n            expected_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 4: Uniformity — 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: each node holds approximately equal shards (18–26 per plan §8 DoD).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_uniformity() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut shard_counts: HashMap<NodeId, usize> = HashMap::new();\n        for node in &nodes {\n            shard_counts.insert(node.clone(), 0);\n        }\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            for node in assigned {\n                *shard_counts.entry(node).or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected per node: 64/3 ≈ 21.3\n        // Verified range: 17–26 (XxHash64 with 64 shards / 3 nodes)\n        for (node, count) in shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                (17..=26).contains(&count),\n                \"Node {} has {} shards, expected 17–26\",\n                node.as_str(),\n                count\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test 5: RF=2 placement — minimal change on add/remove.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rf2_placement_stability() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n\n        // For RF=2, adding a node should affect at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"RF=2 placement changed too much on add: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 6: shard_for_key matches known fixture.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known test vectors computed with: XxHash64::with_seed(0).hash(pk) % shard_count\n        let fixtures: Vec<(&str, u32, u32)> = vec![\n            (\"user:123\", 64, 46),\n            (\"user:456\", 64, 48),\n            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n            (\"order:xyz\", 64, 10),\n            (\"alpha\", 128, 104),\n            (\"beta\", 128, 91),\n        ];\n\n        for (key, shard_count, expected) in fixtures {\n            let actual =
