expected\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test tie-breaking in assign_shard_in_group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tie_breaking() {\n        // Use node IDs that are unlikely but could theoretically collide\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-zzz\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-aaa\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let result = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n\n        // Even if scores were identical (extremely rare), node-aaa should come first\n        // because we sort by node_id ascending for ties\n        assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);\n        // The actual result depends on hash values - we're just verifying determinism\n        let result2 = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n\n        // Different shard_id → different score\n        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n\n        // Different node_id → different score (same shard)\n        let score_0_b = score(0, node_b);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_0_b, \"Same shard, different node should differ\");\n\n        // Verify determinism\n        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests: write_targets, query_group, covering_set ───\n\n    /// P1.3-A1: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes (counting duplicates).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_returns_rg_x_rf_nodes() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 3, 2);\n        // Add 5 nodes to each of 3 groups\n        for i in 0u32..15 {\n            let rg = i / 5;\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let targets = write_targets(0, &topo);\n        // RG=3, RF=2 → 6 nodes total (may include duplicates)\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6, \"write_targets should return RG × RF nodes\");\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A2: write_targets assigns one-per-group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_one_per_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: nodes 0-2, Group 1: nodes 3-5\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let shard_id = 7;\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n\n        // Verify that the subset in group 0 matches assign_shard_in_group\n        let g0 = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let g0_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|n| g0.nodes().contains(n))\n            .collect();\n        let g0_expected = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, g0.nodes(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(\n            g0_targets.len(),\n            g0_expected.len(),\n            \"Group 0 should have exactly RF nodes\"\n        );\n        for node in &g0_expected {\n            assert!(g0_targets.contains(&node), \"Group 0 missing expected node\");\n        }\n\n        // Verify that the subset in group 1 matches assign_shard_in_group\n        let g1 = topo.group(1).unwrap();\n        let g1_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|n| g1.nodes().contains(n))\n            .collect();\n        let g1_expected = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, g1.nodes(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(\n            g1_targets.len(),\n            g1_expected.len(),\n            \"Group 1 should have exactly RF nodes\"\n        );\n        for node in &g1_expected {\n            assert!(g1_targets.contains(&node), \"Group 1 missing expected node\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 1, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 16;\n        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, 2, 0);\n\n        // Verify that every shard is represented in the covering set\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), 2);\n            let selected = &replicas[0]; // query_seq=0 → first replica\n            assert!(\n                covering.contains(selected),\n                \"Shard {}'s selected node {:?} not in covering set\",\n                shard_id,\n                selected\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A4: covering_set size is bounded by Ng (nodes in group).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_size_bound() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(1000, 1, 3);\n        for i in 0u32..5 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let ng = group.node_count();\n        let covering = covering_set(1000, group, 3, 0);\n\n        assert!(\n            covering.len() <= ng,\n            \"covering_set size {} exceeds group node count {}\",\n            covering.len(),\n            ng\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A5: Two identical Topologies produce identical covering_set outputs.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            );\n            topo1.add_node(node.clone());\n            topo2.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let g1 = topo1.group(0).unwrap();\n        let g2 = topo2.group(0).unwrap();\n\n        for query_seq in 0..10 {\n            let c1 = covering_set(64, g1, 2, query_seq);\n            let c2 = covering_set(64, g2, 2, query_seq);\n            // Compare as sets since order may vary due to HashSet iteration\n            let s1: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n            assert_eq!(\n                s1, s2,\n                \"covering_set differs for identical topologies at query_seq={}\",\n                query_seq\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n        let samples = 10_000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n\n        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n            .iter()\n            .map(|&observed| {\n                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n                (diff * diff) / expected\n            })\n            .sum();\n\n        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n        let critical_value = 9.49;\n\n        assert!(\n            chi_square < critical_value,\n            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n            chi_square,\n            critical_value\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n\n        // For each shard, let r1 = &replicas[1];\n            let r2 = &replicas[2];\n\n            assert!(c0.contains(r0), \"query_seq=0 should select first replica\");\n            assert!(c1.contains(r1), \"query_seq=1 should select second replica\");\n            assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":660,"startLine":1,"totalLines":660}}}

the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n151\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n152\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n153\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n154\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n155\t///\n156\t/// Returns };\n249\t\n250\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n251\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n252\t        if let  ).await {\n265\t                        Ok(true) => {\n266\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n267\t                            let wait_duration = state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n270\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n271\t                        }\n272\t                        Ok(false) => {\n273\t state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n276\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n277\t                            (None,         }\n288\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n289\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n290\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n291\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n292\t                }\n293\t            }\n294\t        } else {\n295\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n296\t            (None, None)\n297\t        }\n298\t    } else {\n299\t        // No session header - normal routing\n300\t        (None, None)\n301\t error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n343\t                            return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n344\t                        }\n345\t }\n359\t\n360\t                    let response = response\n361\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n362\t                        .unwrap();\n363\t\n364\t                    tracing::info!(\n365\t                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n366\t                        index = %effective_index,\n367\t primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n413\t            sk.primary_key\n414\t        } else {\n415\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n416\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n417\t        }\n418\t    } else {\n419\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n420\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n421\t    };\n422\t\n423\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n424\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n425\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n426\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n427\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n428\t\n429\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n430\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n431\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n432\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n433\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n434\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n435\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n436\t    };\n437\t\n438\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n439\t    let plan = {\n440\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n441\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n442\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n443\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n444\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n445\t            min_settings_version,\n446\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n447\t            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n448\t        ).entered();\n449\t\n450\t        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n451\t        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n452\t        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n453\t            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n454\t        } else {\n455\t            None\n456\t        };\n457\t\n458\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n459\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n460\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n461\t                &topo,\n462\t                0,\n463\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n464\t                state.config.shards,\n465\t                group,\n466\t                replica_selector_ref,\n467\t            ).await {\n468\t                Some(p) => p,\n469\t                None => {\n470\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n471\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n472\t                    warn!(\n473\t                        pinned_group = group,\n474\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n475\t                    );\n476\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n477\t                }\n478\t            }\n479\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n480\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n481\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n482\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n483\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n484\t                &topo,\n485\t                0,\n486\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n487\t                state.config.shards,\n488\t                &index,\n489\t                floor,\n490\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n491\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n492\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n493\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n494\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n495\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n496\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n497\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n498\t                        })\n499\t                    })\n500\t                },\n501\t                replica_selector_ref,\n502\t            ).await;\n503\t\n504\t            match plan_result {\n505\t                Some(p) => p,\n506\t                None => {\n507\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n508\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n509\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n510\t                        format!(\n511\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n512\t                            floor, index\n513\t                        ),\n514\t                    );\n515\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n516\t                }\n517\t            }\n518\t        } else {\n519\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n520\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n521\t        }\n522\t    };\n523\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n524\t\n525\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n526\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n527\t\n528\t    // Build search request\n529\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n530\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n531\t        query: body.q,\n532\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n533\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n534\t        filter: body.filter,\n535\t        facets: body.facets,\n536\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n537\t        body: body.rest,\n538\t        global_idf: None,\n539\t    };\n540\t\n541\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n542\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n543\t        search_key,\n544\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n545\t    ));\n546\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n547\t\n548\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n549\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n550\t\n551\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n552\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n553\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n554\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n555\t        let query_body = serde_json::to_value(&body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n556\t        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(effective_index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n557\t\n558\t        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n559\t            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n560\t                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n561\t                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n562\t            }\n563\t            Err(_) => {\n564\t                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n565\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n566\t                (None, None)\n567\t            }\n568\t        }\n569\t    } else {\n570\t        (None, None)\n571\t    };\n572\t\n573\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n574\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n575\t        plan,\n576\t        &client,\n577\t        search_req,\n578\t        &topo,\n579\t        policy,\n580\t        &strategy,\n581\t    )\n582\t    .await\n583\t    .map_err(|e| {\n584\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n585\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n586\t    })?;\n587\t\n588\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n589\t    drop(topo);\n590\t\n591\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n592\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n593\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    // Build response body\n597\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n598\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n599\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n600\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n601\t    });\n602\t\n603\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n604\t    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n605\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n606\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n607\t    }\n608\t\n609\t    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n610\t    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n611\t        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n612\t        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n613\t        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n614\t            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n615\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n616\t            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n617\t            debug!(\n618\t                index = %effective_index,\n619\t                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n620\t                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n621\t            );\n622\t        }\n623\t        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n624\t        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n625\t    }\n626\t\n627\t    // Build response with optional headers\n628\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n629\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n630\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n631\t\n632\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n633\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n634\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n635\t    }\n636\t\n637\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n638\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n639\t    if current_version > 0 {\n640\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n641\t    }\n642\t\n643\t    if result.degraded {\n644\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n645\t    }\n646\t\n647\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n648\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n649\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n650\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n651\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n652\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n653\t            .join(\",\");\n654\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n655\t    } else if result.degraded {\n656\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n657\t    }\n658\t\n659\t    let response = response\n660\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n661\t        .unwrap();\n662\t\n663\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n664\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n665\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n666\t    tracing::info!(\n667\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n668\t        index = %effective_index,\n669\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n670\t        node_count = node_count,\n671\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n672\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n673\t        \"search completed\"\n674\t    );\n675\t\n676\t    Ok(response)\n677\t}\n678\t\n679\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n680\t///\n681\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n682\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n683\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n684\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n685\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n686\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n687\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n688\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n689\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n690\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n691\t    let start = Instant::now();\n692\t\n693\t    // Extract session ID if provided\n694\t    let sid = session_id;\n695\t\n696\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n697\t    let source_ip = headers\n698\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n699\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n700\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n701\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n702\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n703\t        .trim()\n704\t        .to_string();\n705\t\n706\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n707\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n708\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n709\t        Err(e) => {\n710\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n711\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n712\t        }\n713\t    };\n714\t\n715\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n716\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n717\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n718\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n719\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n720\t                    if !allowed {\n721\t                        warn!(\n722\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n723\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n724\t                        );\n725\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n726\t                    }\n727\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n728\t                }\n729\t                Err(e) => {\n730\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n731\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n732\t                }\n733\t            }\n734\t        }\n735\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n736\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n737\t            &source_ip,\n738\t            limit,\n739\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n740\t        );\n741\t        if !allowed {\n742\t            warn!(\n743\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n744\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n745\t            );\n746\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n747\t        }\n748\t    }\n749\t\n750\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n751\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n752\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n753\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n754\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n755\t            match strategy {\n756\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n757\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n758\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n759\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n760\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n761\t                        sid,\n762\t                        &state.task_registry,\n763\t                        max_wait,\n764\t                    ).await {\n765\t                        Ok(true) => {\n766\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n767\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n768\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n769\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n770\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n771\t                        }\n772\t                        Ok(false) => {\n773\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n774\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n775\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n776\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n777\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n778\t                        }\n779\t                        Err(_) => {\n780\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n781\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n782\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n783\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n784\t                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n785\t                        }\n786\t                    }\n787\t                }\n788\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n789\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n790\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n791\t                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n792\t                }\n793\t            }\n794\t        } else {\n795\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n796\t            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n797\t        }\n798\t    } else {\n799\t        // No session header - normal routing\n800\t        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n801\t    };\n802\t\n803\t    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n804\t    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n805\t    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n806\t        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n807\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n808\t    })?;\n809\t\n810\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n811\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n812\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n813\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n814\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n815\t                &state.pod_id,\n816\t                primary_target,\n817\t                sk.generation,\n818\t            );\n819\t            sk.primary_key\n820\t        } else {\n821\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n822\t        }\n823\t    } else {\n824\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n825\t    };\n826\t\n827\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n828\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n829\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n830\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n831\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n832\t\n833\t    // Use live topology from shared state\n834\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n835\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n836\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n837\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n838\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n839\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n840\t    };\n841\t\n842\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n843\t    // should have identical shard layout)\n844\t    let plan = {\n845\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n846\t            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n847\t            primary_target = %primary_target,\n848\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n849\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n850\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n851\t            min_settings_version,\n852\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n853\t            target_count = targets.len(),\n854\t        ).entered();\n855\t\n856\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n857\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n858\t                &topo,\n859\t                0,\n860\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n861\t                state.config.shards,\n862\t                group,\n863\t            ) {\n864\t                Some(p) => p,\n865\t                None => {\n866\t                    warn!(\n867\t                        pinned_group = group,\n868\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n869\t                    );\n870\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n871\t                }\n872\t            }\n873\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n874\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n875\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n876\t                &topo,\n877\t                0,\n878\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n879\t                state.config.shards,\n880\t                primary_target,\n881\t                floor,\n882\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n883\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n884\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n885\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n886\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n887\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n888\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n889\t                        })\n890\t                    })\n891\t                },\n892\t            );\n893\t\n894\t            match plan_result {\n895\t                Some(p) => p,\n896\t                None => {\n897\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n898\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n899\t                        format!(\n900\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n901\t                            floor, primary_target\n902\t                        ),\n903\t                    );\n904\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n905\t                }\n906\t            }\n907\t        } else {\n908\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n909\t        }\n910\t    };\n911\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n912\t\n913\t    // Record scatter fan-out size\n914\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n915\t\n916\t    // Build search request for primary target\n917\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n918\t        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n919\t        query: body.q.clone(),\n920\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n921\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n922\t        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n923\t        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n924\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n925\t        body: body.rest.clone(),\n926\t        global_idf: None,\n927\t    };\n928\t\n929\t    // Create node client\n930\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n931\t        search_key,\n932\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n933\t    ));\n934\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n935\t\n936\t    // Use score-based merge strategy\n937\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n938\t\n939\t    // Execute search\n940\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n941\t        plan,\n942\t        &client,\n943\t        search_req,\n944\t        &topo,\n945\t        policy,\n946\t        &strategy,\n947\t    )\n948\t    .await\n949\t    .map_err(|e| {\n950\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n951\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n952\t    })?;\n953\t\n954\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n955\t    drop(topo);\n956\t\n957\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n958\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n959\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n960\t    }\n961\t\n962\t    // Build response body\n963\t    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n964\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n965\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n966\t        \"processingTimeMs\": response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n972\t    }\n973\t\n974\t    // Build response with optional headers\n975\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n976\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n977\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n978\t\n979\t    // Add settings headers\n980\t    if state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n985\t    if current_version > 0 {\n986\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n987\t    }\n988\t\n989\t    if result.degraded {\n990\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n991\t    }\n992\t\n993\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n994\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n995\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n996\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n997\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n998\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n999\t            .join(\",\");\n1000\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n1001\t    } else if result.degraded .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n1007\t        .unwrap();\n1008\t\n1009\t    // Structured log entry\n1010\t    tracing::info!(\n1011\t        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n1012\t        primary_target = %primary_target,\n1013\t        target_count = targets.len(),\n1014\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n1015\t        node_count = node_count,\n1016\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n1017\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n1018\t        \"multi-target search completed\"\n1019\t    );\n1020\t\n1021\t    Ok(response)\n1022\t}\n1023\t\n1024\t/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n1025\t/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n1026\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n1027\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n1028\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n1029\t        if !client_requested_score {\n1030\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n1031\t        }\n1032\t    }\n1033\t}\n1034\t\n1035\t#[cfg(test)]\n1036\tmod tests {\n1037\t    use super::*;\n1038\t\n1039\t    #[test]\n1040\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n1041\t        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n1042\t            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private Search route handler with DFS (Distributed Frequency Search) support.\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\nuse axum::http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode};\nuse axum::response::Response;\nuse axum::Json;\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse miroir_core::session_pinning::WaitStrategy;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, info_span, instrument, warn};\n\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::{AppState, parse_rate_limit};\n\n/// Metrics observer for replica selection events.\n///\n/// Reports selection scores and exploration events to Prometheus.\nstruct MetricsObserver {\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl SelectionObserver for MetricsObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64) {\n        self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_body = serde_json::to_value(&body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(effective_index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return Ok(response);\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            state,\n            headers,\n            sid,\n            client_requested_score,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_body = serde_json::to_value(&body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(effective_index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n            );\n        }\n        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    state: Arc<AppState>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Extract session ID if provided\n    let sid = session_id;\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n
