#[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: true, // Client requested scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 10\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(10)));\n\n        // offset must be 0\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 150 (fetch enough for coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(150)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0f651eb2827f4e069d1b6969","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Supports pluggable merge strategies via the [`MergeStrategy`] trait.\n4\t//! The default strategy is Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::Result;\n7\tuse serde_json::{Map, Value};\n8\tuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n9\tuse    /// Aggregated facet distribution.\n47\t    pub facet_distribution: Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>>,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n50\t    pub ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n63\t// Merge strategy trait\n64\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n65\t\n66\t/// Pluggable merge strategy for combining shard results.\n67\t///\n68\t/// Implementations define how hits from multiple shards are combined\n69\t/// into a single globally-ranked response. The default strategy is\n70\t/// [`RrfStrategy`] (Reciprocal Rank Fusion).\n71\tpub trait MergeStrategy: Send + Sync {\n72\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards into a single response.\n73\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult>;\n74\t\n75\t    /// Strategy name (for logging and `/explain` output).\n76\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Box reference to a merge strategy (for polymorphic dispatch).\n80\tpub type DynMergeStrategy = dyn MergeStrategy;\n81\t\n82\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n83\t// RRF strategy\n84\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n85\t\n86\t/// Default RRF constant k.\n87\t///\n88\t/// The value 60 is recommended in the RRF literature and used by\n89\t/// OpenSearch for hybrid search. Smaller values amplify rank\n90\t/// differences; larger values flatten them.\n91\tpub const DEFAULT_RRF_K: u32 = 60;\n92\t\n93\t/// Reciprocal Rank Fusion merge strategy.\n94\t///\n95\t/// Each document's contribution from a shard is `1 / (k + rank + 1)`\n96\t/// where rank is the 0-based position. Documents appearing in\n97\t/// multiple shards have their contributions summed. Results are\n98\t/// sorted by total RRF score descending, with deterministic\n99\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n101\tpub struct RrfStrategy {\n102\t    k: u32,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl RrfStrategy {\n106\t    /// Create a new RRF strategy with the given k constant.\n107\t    pub fn new(k: u32) -> Self {\n108\t        Self { k: k.max(1) }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Create with the default k=60.\n112\t    pub fn default_strategy() -> Self {\n113\t        Self::new(DEFAULT_RRF_K)\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    /// Return the configured k value.\n117\t    pub fn k(&self) -> u32 {\n118\t        self.k\n119\t    }\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\timpl Default for RrfStrategy {\n123\t    fn default() -> Self {\n124\t        Self::default_strategy()\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl MergeStrategy for RrfStrategy {\n129\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n130\t        rrf_merge(&self.k, input)\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n134\t        \"rrf\"\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// A document with its accumulated RRF score.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n140\tstruct RRFDocument {\n141\t    /// Accumulated RRF score across all shards.\n142\t    rrf_score: f64,\n143\t\n144\t    /// Primary key for tie-breaking.\n145\t    primary_key: String,\n146\t\n147\t    /// The hit document (JSON object) from the highest-ranking shard.\n148\t    hit: Map<String, Value>,\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\timpl PartialEq for RRFDocument {\n152\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n153\t        self.rrf_score == other.rrf_score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n154\t    }\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\timpl Eq for RRFDocument {}\n158\t\n159\timpl PartialOrd for RRFDocument {\n160\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {\n161\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n162\t    }\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl Ord for RRFDocument {\n166\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\n167\t        // Primary sort: RRF score descending (higher score = better rank)\n168\t        match self.rrf_score.partial_cmp(&other.rrf_score) {\n169\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n170\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n171\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n172\t            }\n173\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n174\t            None => {\n175\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n176\t                if self.rrf_score.is_nan() && !other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n177\t                    Ordering::Less\n178\t                } else if !self.rrf_score.is_nan() && other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n179\t                    Ordering::Greater\n180\t                } else {\n181\t                    Ordering::Equal\n182\t                }\n183\t            }\n184\t        }\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Merge search results using the default RRF strategy (k=60).\n189\t///\n190\t/// This is a convenience wrapper around [`RrfStrategy`] for callers\n191\t/// that don't need to customise the strategy.\n192\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n193\t    rrf_merge(&DEFAULT_RRF_K, input)\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t/// Merge search results with a specific strategy.\n197\t///\n198\t/// Use this when the strategy is selected from config or when you\n199\t/// need a non-default RRF k value.\n200\tpub fn merge_with_strategy(\n201\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n202\t    input: MergeInput,\n203\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n204\t    strategy.merge(input)\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t/// Core RRF merge implementation.\n208\tfn rrf_merge(k: &u32, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n209\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n210\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n211\t    let mut degraded = false;\n212\t\n213\t    // Collect all hits with their ranks from all shards.\n214\t    // Use a map to aggregate RRF scores for documents appearing in multiple shards.\n215\t    let mut rrf_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, RRFDocument> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n216\t\n217\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n218\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n219\t\n220\t        // Check for degraded response.\n221\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n222\t            degraded = true;\n223\t            continue;\n224\t        }\n225\t\n226\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n227\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n228\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n229\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n230\t            }\n231\t        }\n232\t\n233\t        // Extract processing time.\n234\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n235\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n236\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n237\t            }\n238\t        }\n239\t\n240\t        // Extract hits with ranks (position in shard's results).\n241\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n242\t            for (rank, hit) in hits.iter().enumerate() {\n243\t                if let Value::Object(ref map) = hit {\n244\t                    let map = map.clone();\n245\t\n246\t                    // Extract primary key for deduplication.\n247\t                    let primary_key = map\n248\t                        .get(\"id\")\n249\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n250\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n251\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n252\t                        .to_string();\n253\t\n254\t                    // RRF contribution: 1 / (k + rank + 1)\n255\t                    // rank is 0-based, so +1 converts to 1-based position.\n256\t                    let rrf_contribution = 1.0 / ((*k as f64) + (rank as f64) + 1.0);\n257\t\n258\t                    // Aggregate RRF scores across shards.\n259\t                    use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;\n260\t                    match rrf_map.entry(primary_key.clone()) {\n261\t                        Entry::Vacant(e) => {\n262\t                            e.insert(RRFDocument {\n263\t                                rrf_score: rrf_contribution,\n264\t                                primary_key,\n265\t                                hit: map,\n266\t                            });\n267\t                        }\n268\t                        Entry::Occupied(mut e) => {\n269\t                            // Document appears in multiple shards: sum RRF contributions.\n270\t                            e.get_mut().rrf_score += rrf_contribution;\n271\t                        }\n272\t                    }\n273\t                }\n274\t            }\n275\t        }\n276\t    }\n277\t\n278\t    // Convert map to vec and sort by RRF score descending.\n279\t    let mut merged_docs: Vec<_> = rrf_map.into_values().collect();\n280\t    merged_docs.sort();\n281\t\n282\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n283\t    let skip = input.offset;\n284\t    let take = input.limit;\n285\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = merged_docs\n286\t        .into_iter()\n287\t        .skip(skip)\n288\t        .take(take)\n289\t        .collect();\n290\t\n291\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n292\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n293\t    for mut doc in paginated_hits {\n294\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested (RRF doesn't use original scores).\n295\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n296\t            doc.hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n297\t        }\n298\t\n299\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n300\t        doc.hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n301\t\n302\t        hits.push(Value::Object(doc.hit));\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Merge facets.\n306\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n307\t\n308\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n309\t        hits,\n310\t        facet_distribution,\n311\t        estimated_total_hits,\n312\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n313\t        degraded,\n314\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n315\t    })\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t/// Score-based merge strategy (OP#4 global-IDF).\n319\t///\n320\t/// This merge strategy is correct **only when** the preflight phase has\n321\t/// provided global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts\n322\t/// all hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic\n323\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n324\t///\n325\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n326\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n327\t/// distributions.\n328\t///\n329\t/// Use with [`dfs_query_then_fetch_search`] in the scatter module.\n330\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\n331\tpub struct ScoreMergeStrategy;\n332\t\n333\timpl ScoreMergeStrategy {\n334\t    /// Create a new score-based merge strategy.\n335\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n336\t        Self\n337\t    }\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\timpl Default for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n341\t    fn default() -> Self {\n342\t        Self::new()\n343\t    }\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\timpl MergeStrategy for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n347\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n348\t        score_merge(input)\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n352\t        \"score\"\n353\t    }\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\t/// Core score-based merge implementation (OP#4 global-IDF).\n357\t///\n358\t/// This merge strategy is correct when the preflight phase has provided\n359\t/// global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts all\n360\t/// hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic tie-breaking\n361\t/// on primary key.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n364\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n365\t/// distributions.\n366\tfn score_merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n367\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n368\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n369\t    let mut degraded = false;\n370\t    let mut seen_pks = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n371\t    let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n372\t\n373\t    // Collect all hits from all shards, deduplicating by primary key.\n374\t    // Dedup is needed because execute_scatter may push the same node response\n375\t    // once per shard when multiple shards map to the same node.\n376\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n377\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n378\t\n379\t        // Check for degraded response.\n380\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n381\t            degraded = true;\n382\t            continue;\n383\t        }\n384\t\n385\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n386\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n387\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n388\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t\n392\t        // Extract processing time.\n393\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n394\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n395\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n396\t            }\n397\t        }\n398\t\n399\t        // Extract hits.\n400\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n401\t            for hit in hits {\n402\t                if let Value::Object(map) = hit {\n403\t                    let pk = map\n404\t                        .get(\"id\")\n405\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n406\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n407\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n408\t                        .to_string();\n409\t                    if seen_pks.insert(pk) {\n410\t                        all_hits.push(map.clone());\n411\t                    }\n412\t                }\n413\t            }\n414\t        }\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking.\n418\t    all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| {\n419\t        let score_a = a.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n420\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n421\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n422\t        let score_b = b.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n423\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n424\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n425\t\n426\t        // Extract primary keys for tie-breaking.\n427\t        let pk_a = a.get(\"id\")\n428\t            .or_else(|| a.get(\"pk\"))\n429\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n430\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n431\t        let pk_b = b.get(\"id\")\n432\t            .or_else(|| b.get(\"pk\"))\n433\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n434\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n435\t\n436\t        // Primary sort: score descending (higher score = better rank)\n437\t        match score_a.partial_cmp(&score_b) {\n438\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n439\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n440\t                pk_a.cmp(pk_b)\n441\t            }\n442\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n443\t            None => {\n444\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n445\t                if score_a.is_nan() && !score_b.is_nan() {\n446\t                    Ordering::Less\n447\t                } else if !score_a.is_nan() && score_b.is_nan() {\n448\t                    Ordering::Greater\n449\t                } else {\n450\t                    Ordering::Equal\n451\t                }\n452\t            }\n453\t        }\n454\t    });\n455\t\n456\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n457\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = all_hits\n458\t        .into_iter()\n459\t        .skip(input.offset)\n460\t        .take(input.limit)\n461\t        .collect();\n462\t\n463\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n464\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n465\t    for mut hit in paginated_hits {\n466\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested.\n467\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n468\t            hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n469\t        }\n470\t\n471\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n472\t        hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n473\t\n474\t        hits.push(Value::Object(hit));\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    // Merge facets.\n478\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n479\t\n480\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n481\t        hits,\n482\t        facet_distribution,\n483\t        estimated_total_hits,\n484\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n485\t        degraded,\n486\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n487\t    })\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/// Merge facet distributions from multiple shards.\n491\t///\n492\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable ordering (deterministic serialization).\n493\tfn merge_facets(\n494\t    shard_pages: &[ShardHitPage],\n495\t    requested_facets: Option<&[String]>,\n496\t) -> Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>> {\n497\t    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n498\t\n499\t    for shard_page in shard_pages {\n500\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n501\t\n502\t        // Meilisearch uses \"facetDistribution\" for facet results.\n503\t        if let Some(Value::Object(facets)) = body.get(\"facetDistribution\") {\n504\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n505\t                // Skip if not requested (if a filter was provided).\n506\t                if let Some(requested) = requested_facets {\n507\t                    if !requested.iter().any(|f| f == facet_name) {\n508\t                        continue;\n509\t                    }\n510\t                }\n511\t\n512\t                if let Value::Object(values_map) = facet_values {\n513\t                    let merged_facet = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n514\t                    for (value, count) in values_map {\n515\t                        if let Value::Number(n) = count {\n516\t                            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n517\t                                *merged_facet.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += n;\n518\t                            }\n519\t                        }\n520\t                    }\n521\t                }\n522\t            }\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    if merged.is_empty() {\n527\t        None\n528\t    } else {\n529\t        Some(merged)\n530\t    }\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t#[cfg(test)]\n534\tmod tests {\n535\t    use super::*;\n536\t    use serde_json::json;\n537\t\n538\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n539\t    // Trait / strategy tests\n540\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n541\t\n542\t    #[test]\n543\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_default_matches_free_function() {\n544\t        let input = MergeInput {\n545\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n546\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0)],\n547\t                100,\n548\t                15,\n549\t            )],\n550\t            offset: 0,\n551\t            limit: 10,\n552\t            client_requested_score: false,\n553\t            facets: None,\n554\t        failed_shards: }\n564\t\n565\t    #[test]\n566\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_custom_k() {\n567\t        // With k=1, rank 0 gets 1/(1+0+1) = 0.5\n568\t        // With k=60, rank 0 gets 1/(60+0+1) ≈ 0.0164\n569\t        // Both should produce the same ordering for a single shard.\n570\t        let input = MergeInput {\n571\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n572\t                vec![make_hit(\"a\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"b\", 0.5, 0)],\n573\t                50,\n574\t                10,\n575\t            )],\n576\t            offset: 0,\n577\t            limit: 10,\n578\t            client_requested_score: false,\n579\t            facets: None,\n580\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n581\t        };\n582\t\n583\t        let strategy_k1 = RrfStrategy::new(1);\n584\t        let }\n588\t\n589\t    #[test]\n590\t    fn test_merge_with_strategy_dispatches() {\n591\t        let input = MergeInput {\n592\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n593\t    fn make_hit(id: &str, score: f64, shard: u32) -> Value {\n621\t        json!({\n622\t            \"id\": id,\n623\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n624\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n625\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n626\t        })\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n630\t    fn make_hit_ranked(id: &str, shard: u32) -> Value {\n631\t        json!({\n632\t            \"id\": id,\n633\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n634\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.5, // RRF ignores score, only rank matters\n635\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n636\t        })\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    fn make_shard_response(\n640\t        hits: Vec<Value>,\n641\t        total_hits: u64,\n642\t        processing_time: u64,\n643\t    ) -> ShardHitPage {\n644\t        ShardHitPage {\n645\t            body: json!({\n646\t                \"hits\": hits,\n647\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total_hits,\n648\t                \"processingTimeMs\": processing_time,\n649\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n650\t            }),\n651\t        }\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    #[test]\n655\t    fn test_merge_basic() {\n656\t        let input = MergeInput {\n657\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n658\t                vec![\n659\t                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n660\t                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n661\t                ],\n662\t                100,\n663\t                15,\n664\t            )],\n665\t #[test]\n686\t    fn test_merge_global_sort() {\n687\t        let input = MergeInput {\n688\t            shard_hits: vec![\n689\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n690\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 1)], 50, 10),\n691\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 2)], 50, 10),\n692\t            ],\n693\t            offset: 0,\n694\t            limit: 10,\n695\t            client_requested_score: true,\n696\t            facets: None,\n697\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n698\t        };\n699\t\n700\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n701\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 3);\n702\t\n703\t        // RRF: all docs are at rank 0 in their respective shards.\n704\t        // With equal ranks, tie-break by primary key (alphabetically).\n705\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n706\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n707\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n708\t    }\n709\t\n710\t    #[test]\n711\t    fn test_merge_rrf_rank_ordering() {\n712\t        // Test RRF: higher rank (lower position) contributes more to RRF score.\n713\t        // shard0: [low_rank_doc (rank 0), mid_rank_doc (rank 10)]\n714\t        // shard1: [high_rank_doc (rank 0)]\n715\t        // shard2: [mid_rank_doc (rank 0) - same doc appears in two shards!]\n716\t        //\n717\t        // Expected RRF scores:\n718\t        // - low_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard0)\n719\t        // - high_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard1)\n720\t        // - mid_rank_doc: 1/(60+10+1) + 1/(60+0+1) = 1/71 + 1/61 (rank 10 in shard0, rank 0 in shard2)\n721\t        //\n722\t        // mid_rank_doc should win because it appears in multiple shards.\n723\t\n724\t        let mut shard0_hits = vec![];\n725\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n726\t        let mut shard2_hits = vec![];\n727\t\n728\t        // Build shard0: low_rank_doc at position 0, mid_rank_doc at position 10\n729\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"low_rank_doc\", 0.1, 0));\n730\t        for i in 0..9 {\n731\t            shard0_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_0_{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n732\t        }\n733\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.2, 0));\n734\t\n735\t        // shard1: high_rank_doc at position 0\n736\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"high_rank_doc\", 0.3, 1));\n737\t\n738\t        // shard2: mid_rank_doc at position 0 (same doc appears again!)\n739\t        shard2_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.4, 2));\n740\t\n741\t        let input = MergeInput {\n742\t            shard_hits: vec![\n743\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 100, 10),\n744\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 100, 10),\n745\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 100, 10),\n746\t            ],\n747\t            offset: 0,\n748\t            limit: 10,\n749\t            client_requested_score: false,\n750\t            facets: None,\n751\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n752\t        };\n753\t\n754\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n755\t\n756\t        // mid_rank_doc should be first because it appears in multiple shards\n757\t        // (RRF contributions sum: rank 10 in shard0 + rank 0 in shard2)\n758\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"mid_rank_doc\");\n759\t\n760\t        // low_rank_doc and high_rank_doc both at rank 0 in their shards\n761\t        // Tie-break by primary key alphabetically\n762\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"high_rank_doc\");\n763\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"low_rank_doc\");\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    #[test]\n767\t    fn test_merge_rrf_duplicate_handling() {\n768\t        // Test that the same document appearing in multiple shards\n769\t        // gets its RRF score summed.\n770\t        //\n771\t        // doc1 appears at rank 0 in shard0 and rank 5 in shard1\n772\t        // doc2 appears at rank 0 in shard2\n773\t        //\n774\t        // RRF(doc1) = 1/(60+0+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 1/61 + 1/66\n775\t        // RRF(doc2) = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61\n776\t        //\n777\t        // doc1 should rank higher.\n778\t\n779\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)];\n780\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n781\t        let shard2_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 2)];\n782\t\n783\t        // Add filler hits to shard1 to make doc1 appear at rank 5\n784\t        for i in 0..5 {\n785\t            shard1_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_1_{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n786\t        }\n787\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.2, 1));\n788\t\n789\t        let input = MergeInput {\n790\t            shard_hits: vec![\n791\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n792\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n793\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n794\t            ],\n795\t            offset: 0,\n796\t            limit: 10,\n797\t            client_requested_score: false,\n798\t            facets: None,\n799\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n800\t        };\n801\t\n802\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n803\t\n804\t        // doc1 should rank higher despite lower score because\n805\t        // it appears in multiple shards and gets RRF contribution boost\n806\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n807\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n808\t    }\n809\t\n810\t    #[test]\n811\t    fn test_merge_offset_limit() {\n812\t        let input = MergeInput {\n813\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n814\t                vec![\n815\t                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, field when requested.\n841\t        let input = MergeInput {\n842\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n843\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n844\t // Test that RRF ordering is based on rank, not score.\n864\t        // Even though doc3 has highest score, it's ranked lower in its shard.\n865\t        //\n866\t        // shard0: doc2 at rank 0 with score 0.9 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n867\t        // shard1: doc1 at rank 0 with score 0.1 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n868\t        // shard2: doc3 at rank 2 (position 2) with score 1.0 (lower rank)\n869\t        //\n870\t        // RRF scores:\n871\t        // - doc1: 1/61 (rank 0)\n872\t        // - doc2: 1/61 (rank 0)\n873\t        // - filler: 1/62 (rank 1)\n874\t        // - doc3: 1/63 (rank 2)\n875\t        //\n876\t        // Ordering: doc1, doc2 (tie-break alphabetically), filler, then doc3\n877\t\n878\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 0)]; // High score, rank 0\n879\t        let shard1_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)]; // Low score, rank 0\n880\t        let shard2_hits = vec![\n881\t            make_hit(\"filler\", 0.5, 2),\n882\t            make_hit(\"filler2\", 0.5, 2),\n883\t            make_hit(\"doc3\", 1.0, 2),  // Highest score, but rank 2\n884\t        ];\n885\t\n886\t        let input = MergeInput {\n887\t            shard_hits: vec![\n888\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n889\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n890\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n891\t            ],\n892\t            offset: 0,\n893\t            limit: 10,\n894\t            client_requested_score: true,\n895\t            facets: None,\n896\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n897\t        };\n898\t\n899\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n900\t\n901\t        // doc1 and doc2 both at rank 0, tie-break alphabetically\n902\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n903\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n904\t        // filler and filler2 at rank 1\n905\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler\");\n906\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler2\");\n907\t        // doc3 at rank 2, comes last despite highest score\n908\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n909\t    }\n910\t\n911\t    #[test]\n912\t    fn test_merge_strips_miroir_fields() {\n913\t        let input = #[test]\n1037\t    fn test_merge_estimated_total_hits_sum() {\n1038\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1039\t            shard_hits: vec![\n1040\t                make_shard_response(vec![], 100, limit=50 result.\n1170\t        let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n1171\t        for i in 0..50 {\n1172\t            all_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"doc{:02}\", i), (50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0));\n1173\t        }\n1174\t\n1175\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1176\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(all_hits, 50, 10)],\n1177\t            offset: 0,\n1178\t            limit: 50,\n1179\t            client_requested_score: false,\n1180\t            facets: None,\n1181\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1182\t        };\n1183\t\n1184\t        let full_result = {\n1189\t            let page_input = MergeInput {\n1190\t                shard_hits: input.shard_hits.clone(),\n1191\t                offset: page * 10,\n1192\t                limit: 10,\n1193\t -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1211\t    // RRF correctness properties\n1212\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1213\t\n1214\t    #[test]\n1215\t    fn test_rrf_cross_shard_replication_boost() {\n1216\t        // Key RRF property: a document appearing in multiple shards\n1217\t        // gets a higher merged score than a single-shard document,\n1218\t        // even if it ranks lower within each shard.\n1219\t        //\n1220\t        // doc-replicated: rank 5 in shard0, rank 5 in shard1\n1221\t        //   RRF = 1/(60+5+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 2/66 ≈ 0.0303\n1222\t        // doc-single: rank 0 in shard2\n1223\t        //   RRF = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n1224\t        //\n1225\t        // doc-replicated should rank higher.\n1226\t\n1227\t        let mut shard0 = vec![];\n1228\t        let mut shard1 = vec![];\n1229\t        for i in 0..5 {\n1230\t            shard0.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler0-{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n1231\t            shard1.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler1-{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n1232\t        }\n1233\t        shard0.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 0));\n1234\t        shard1.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 1));\n1235\t\n1236\t        let shard2 = vec![make_hit(\"doc-single\", 0.99, 2)];\n1237\t\n1238\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1239\t        let result = strategy\n1240\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1241\t                shard_hits: vec![\n1242\t                    make_shard_response(shard0, 100, 10),\n1243\t                    make_shard_response(shard1, 100, 10),\n1244\t                    make_shard_response(shard2, 100, 10),\n1245\t                ],\n1246\t                offset: 0,\n1247\t                limit: 20,\n1248\t                client_requested_score: false,\n1249\t                facets: None,\n1250\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1251\t            })\n1252\t            .unwrap();\n1253\t\n1254\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n1255\t        let rep_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-replicated\").unwrap();\n1256\t        let single_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-single\").unwrap();\n1257\t        assert!(\n1258\t            rep_pos < single_pos,\n1259\t            \"Replicated doc at pos {} should rank above single doc at pos {}\",\n1260\t            rep_pos,\n1261\t            single_pos\n1262\t        );\n1263\t    }\n1264\t\n1265\t    #[test]\n1266\t    fn test_rrf_immune_to_score_scale() {\n1267\t        // RRF uses rank only: scores of 0.001 vs 0.999 don't affect ordering.\n1268\t        // Two shards with wildly different score ranges should produce the\n1269\t        // same merge as two shards with uniform scores.\n1270\t        let shard_a = ShardHitPage {\n1271\t            body: json!({\n1272\t                \"hits\": [\n1273\t                    {\"id\": \"a1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.001},\n1274\t                    {\"id\": \"a2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.002},\n1275\t                ],\n1276\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n1277\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1278\t            }),\n1279\t        };\n1280\t        let shard_b = ShardHitPage {\n1281\t            body: json!({\n1282\t                \"hits\": [\n1283\t                    {\"id\": \"b1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.999},\n1284\t                    {\"id\": \"b2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.998},\n1285\t                ],\n1286\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n1287\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n1288\t            }),\n1289\t        };\n1290\t\n1291\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1292\t        let result = strategy\n1293\t            .merge(MergeInput {\n1294\t                shard_hits: vec![shard_a, shard_b],\n1295\t                offset: 0,\n1296\t                limit: 10,\n1297\t                client_requested_score: false,\n1298\t                facets: None,\n1299\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1300\t            })\n1301\t            .unwrap();\n1302\t\n1303\t        // All at rank 0 or 1 — tie-break alphabetically within each rank tier.\n1304\t        // rank 0: a1, b1 → sort by id → a1, b1\n1305\t        // rank 1: a2, b2 → sort by id → a2, b2\n1306\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n1307\t        assert_eq!(ids, vec![\"a1\", \"b1\", \"a2\", \"b2\"]);\n1308\t    }\n1309\t\n1310\t    #[test]\n1311\t    fn test_rrf_deterministic_with_same_input() {\n1312\t        // RRF merge is a pure function: same input always produces same output.\n1313\t        let shard = make_shard_response(\n1314\t            (0..100).map(|i| make_hit(&format!(\"doc{}\", i), (100 #[test]\n1367\t    fn test_rrf_document_equality() {\n1368\t        use super::Ordering;\n1369\t        let a = super::RRFDocument {\n1370\t            rrf_score: 1.0,\n1371\t            primary_key: assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&nan_b), Ordering::Equal);\n1400\t\n1401\t        // NaN vs real: NaN is Less\n1402\t        let real = super::RRFDocument {\n1403\t            rrf_score: 1.0,\n1404\t            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n1405\t            hit: Map::new(),\n1406\t        };\n1407\t        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&real), Ordering::Less);\n1408\t        assert_eq!(real.cmp(&nan_a), preflight.\n1591\t    ///\n1592\t    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n1593\t    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n1594\t    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n1595\t    ///\n1596\t    /// Without global IDF:\n1597\t    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n1598\t    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n1599\t    ///\n1600\t    /// With global IDF:\n1601\t    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n1602\t    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n1603\t    ///\n1604\t    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n1605\t    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n1606\t    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n1607\t    ///\n1608\t    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n1609\t    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n1610\t    ///\n1611\t    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n1612\t    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n1613\t    #[test]\n1614\t    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n1615\t        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n1616\t        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n1617\t\n1618\t        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n1619\t        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n1620\t            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n1621\t            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n1622\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n1623\t        });\n1624\t\n1625\t        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n1626\t        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n1627\t            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n1628\t            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n1629\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n1630\t        });\n1631\t\n1632\t        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n1633\t        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n1634\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1635\t            shard_hits: vec![\n1636\t                ShardHitPage {\n1637\t                    body: json!({\n1638\t                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n1639\t                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n1640\t                        \"processingTimeMs\": Vec::new(),\n1656\t        };\n1657\t\n1658\t        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n1659\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n1660\t\n1661\t        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n1662\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n1663\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n1664\t\n1665\t        // Scores should be preserved\n1666\t        assert_eq!(\n1667\t            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n1668\t            Some(0.85)\n1669\t        );\n1670\t        assert_eq!(\n1671\t            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n1672\t            Some(0.75)\n1673\t        );\n1674\t    }\n1675\t\n1676\t    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n1677\t    ///\n1678\t    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n1679\t    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n1680\t    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n1681\t    #[test]\n1682\t    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n1683\t        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n1684\t        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n1685\t\n1686\t assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n1741\t    }\n1742\t\n1743\t    #[test]\n1744\t    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n1745\t        let strategy = -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1761\t    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n1762\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1763\t\n1764\t    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n1765\t    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n1766\t    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n1767\t    ///\n1768\t    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n1769\t    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). ///\n1773\t    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n1774\t    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n1775\t    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n1776\t    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n1777\t    ///\n1778\t    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n1779\t    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n1780\t    #[test]\n1781\t    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n1782\t        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n1783\t        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n1784\t        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n1785\t            vec![\n1786\t                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n1787\t                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n1788\t                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),  ],\n1792\t            93_000,\n1793\t            10,\n1794\t        );\n1795\t\n1796\t        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n1797\t        // can appear at rank 0 score\n1801\t                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n1802\t            ],\n1803\t            10,\n1804\t            2,\n1805\t        );\n1806\t\n1807\t        let strategy = })\n1817\t            .unwrap();\n1818\t\n1819\t        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n1820\t            .hits\n1821\t            .iter()\n1822\t            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n1823\t            .collect();\n1824\t\n1825\t        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n1826\t        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n1827\t        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n1828\t        //\n1829\t        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n1830\t        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n1837\t\n1838\t        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n1839\t        let     );\n1847\t    }\n1848\t\n1849\t    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n1850\t    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n1851\t    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n1852\t        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n1853\t            .iter()\n1854\t            .enumerate()\n1855\t            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n1856\t            .collect();\n1857\t        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n1858\t            .iter()\n1859\t            .enumerate()\n1860\t            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n1861\t            .collect();\n1862\t\n1863\t        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n1864\t            .keys()\n1865\t            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n1866\t            .map(|k| *k)\n1867\t            .collect();\n1868\t\n1869\t        if common.len() < 2 {\n1870\t            return 0.0;\n1871\t        }\n1872\t\n1873\t        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n1874\t        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n1875\t        let n = common.len();\n1876\t        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n1877\t        let concordant = total - discordant;\n1878\t        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n1879\t    }\n1880\t\n1881\t    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n1882\t        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n1883\t            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n1884\t        }\n1885\t        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n1886\t        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n1887\t        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n1888\t\n1889\t        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n1890\t        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n1891\t        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n1892\t\n1893\t        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n1894\t            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n1895\t                merged.push(left[i]);\n1896\t                i += 1;\n1897\t            } else {\n1898\t                merged.push(right[j]);\n1899\t                inv += left.len() - i;\n1900\t                j += 1;\n1901\t            }\n1902\t        }\n1903\t        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n1904\t        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n1905\t        (merged, inv)\n1906\t    }\n1907\t\n1908\t    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n1909\t    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n1910\t    ///\n1911\t    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n1912\t    #[test]\n1913\t    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n1914\t        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n1915\t\n1916\t        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n1917\t        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n1918\t        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n1919\t        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n1920\t        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n1921\t\n1922\t        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n1923\t        for (shard_id, &size) in
