P2.3 Search read path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the scatter-gather + merge + group selection implementation.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit (proves merger + fan-out correctness)\n//! - Facet counts sum correctly across shards\n//! - Paging: 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps\n//! - With one node down and RF=2: search still covers all shards (tests fall-back within the group)\n//! - With one group fully down: search uses the other group; response is not X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=... stamped when a shard has zero live replicas\n\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{plan_search_scatter, MockNodeClient, SearchRequest};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, Topology};\nuse serde_json::json;\n\n/// Create a 3-node topology with 2 replica groups and RF=2.\n///\n/// Group 0: node-0, node-1\n/// Group 1: node-2\nfn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 1));\n    topo\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A1: Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit.\n///\n/// This proves that:\n/// - Scatter correctly fans out to all nodes in the covering set\n/// - Merge correctly deduplicates documents across shards (using RRF)\n///\n/// Note: This test simulates a document that exists on multiple shards\n/// (replicated data). RRF deduplicates by primary key.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_returns_exactly_one_hit() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards, 1 group, RF=1 for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // All three nodes return the SAME document (same primary key = \"unique-doc-123\")\n    // This simulates a document that is replicated across multiple shards\n    let response = json!({\n        \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"unique-doc-123\", \"title\": \"Unique Result\"}],\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n    });\n\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), response.clone());\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), response.clone());\n    client.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), response);\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"unique keyword xyz123\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Use RRF strategy which deduplicates by primary key\n    let strategy = miroir_core::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Should have exactly 1 hit after deduplication\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1, \"Should deduplicate to 1 hit\");\n    assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"unique-doc-123\");\n    assert!(!result.degraded);\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A2: Facet counts sum correctly across shards.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_facet_counts_sum_correctly() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Node 0 returns category facet counts\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"electronics\": 50, \"books\": 30}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 1 returns category facet counts (overlapping with node 0)\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 80,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"electronics\": 40, \"clothing\": 25}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 2 returns category facet counts\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 60,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"books\": 20, \"clothing\": 15}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into()]),\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &ScoreMergeStrategy::new(),\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n    let category = facets.get(\"category\").unwrap();\n\n    // Verify counts are summed correctly across 3 shards\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"electronics\"), Some(&90)); // 50 + 40\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"books\"), Some(&50));      // 30 + 20\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"clothing\"), Some(&40));   // 25 + 15\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A3: Paging - 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps.\n///\n/// Uses RRF which deduplicates by primary key.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_no_dupes_or_gaps() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(10, 1, 1); // 10 shards, 1 group, RF=1\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        // Only 3 nodes to ensure simple routing\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i)),\n            format!(\"http://node-{}:7700\", i),\n            0,\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 10, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Each node returns unique documents - use disjoint ID ranges to avoid collision\n    // Node 0: docs 0-16, Node 1: docs 17-33, Node 2: docs 34-49\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let start = i * 17;\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for j in 0..17 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"doc-{:03}\", start + j),\n                \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", start + j),\n                \"_rankingScore\": (100.0 - (start + j) as f64) / 100.0,\n            }));\n        }\n\n        client.responses.insert(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i)),\n            json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 17,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Use RRF strategy for deduplication\n    let strategy = miroir_core::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n\n    // Fetch all 5 pages (50 total documents, 10 per page)\n    let mut all_ids = Vec::new();\n    for page in 0..5 {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: page * 10,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false,\n            body: json!({}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n            plan.clone(),\n            &client,\n            req,\n            &topo,\n            UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n            &strategy,\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 10, \"Page {} should have 10 hits\", page);\n        for hit in &result.hits {\n            let id = hit.get(\"id\").unwrap().as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n            all_ids.push(id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify no duplicates\n    let unique_ids: std::collections::HashSet<_> = all_ids.iter().collect();\n    assert_eq!(unique_ids.len(), 50, \"All IDs should be unique, got {}\", unique_ids.len());\n\n    // Verify all docs from doc-000 to doc-049 are present\n    for i in 0..50 {\n        let expected = format!(\"doc-{:03}\", i);\n        assert!(all_ids.contains(&expected), \"Missing document {}\", expected);\n    }\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A4: With one node down and RF=2, search still covers all shards.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_node_down_rf2_covers_all_shards() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 1, 2); // 1 group, RF=2\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Node 0 returns valid data\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Doc 1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }),\n    );\n\n    // Node 1 is down (timeout)\n    client.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout);\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // With RF=2, each shard has 2 replicas. When one fails, the other succeeds.\n    assert!(result.partial, \"Result should be partial when one node fails\");\n    assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have results from surviving replicas\");\n    assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have some failed shards\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A5: With one group fully down, search uses the other group (fallback).\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_group_down_fallback_succeeds_not_degraded() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 1); // 2 groups, RF=1\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0\".into()), \"http://g0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1\".into()), \"http://g1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 16, None).await; // query_seq=0 → group 0\n    assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Group 0 node is down\n    client.errors.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-g0\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n\n    // Group 1 node is healthy\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-g1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\"}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Fallback to group 1 should succeed completely\n    assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should provide complete results\");\n    assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3-A6: X-Miroir-Degraded header includes actual shard IDs.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_degraded_header_includes_shard_ids() {\n    let topo = make_test_topology();\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // One node succeeds\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc-1\"}],\n NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n    client.errors.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout,\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::execute_scatter(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    assert!(result.partial, \"Result should be partial\");\n    assert!(!result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"Should have failed shards\");\n\n    // Verify failed_shards contains actual shard IDs\n    let mut shard_ids: Vec<_> = result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n    shard_ids.sort();\n    assert!(!shard_ids.is_empty(), \"Should have at least one failed shard\");\n\n    // Verify we can format the header value correctly: \"shards=3,7,11\"\n    let header_value = format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids.iter().map(|id| id.to_string()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\",\"));\n    assert!(header_value.starts_with(\"shards=\"), \"Header should start with 'shards='\");\n}\n\n/// P2.3: Integration test - end-to-end search with all features.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_search_read_path_integration() {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1); // 3 shards for simplicity\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n\n    let mut client = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Set up realistic responses with hits, facets, and scores\n    // Each node returns different documents (no overlap)\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"First\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.95},\n                {\"id\": \"doc-2\", \"title\": \"Second\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.85},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 50,\n       NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-3\", \"title\": \"Third\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.90},\n                {\"id\": \"doc-4\", \"title\": \"Fourth\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.80},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 40,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 8,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"tech\": 25, \"science\": 15}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    client.responses.insert(\n        NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()),\n        json!({\n            \"hits\": [\n                {\"id\": \"doc-5\", \"title\": \"Fifth\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.88},\n            ],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 30,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 12,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"category\": {\"tech\": 20, \"science\": 10}\n            }\n        }),\n    );\n\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n        query: Some(\"integration test\".into()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 10,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into()]),\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = miroir_core::scatter::scatter_gather_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &ScoreMergeStrategy::new(),\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify results\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 5); // All 5 unique docs\n    assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 120); // Sum of all totals\n    assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 12); // Max of 10, 8, 12\n    assert!(!result.degraded); // All nodes succeeded\n\n    // Verify facets are summed correctly\n    let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n    let category = facets.get(\"category\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"tech\"), Some(&75)); // 30 + 25 + 20\n    assert_eq!(category.get(\"science\"), Some(&45)); // 20 + 15 + 10\n\n    // Verify hits are sorted by score descending\n    for i in 0..4 {\n        let score_i = result.hits[i]\n            .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap();\n        let score_j = result.hits[i + 1]\n            .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            score_i >= score_j,\n            \"Hits should be sorted by score descending: {} >= {}\",\n            score_i,\n            score_j\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n/// P2.3: Verify showRankingScore is injected unconditionally.\n#[test]\nfn test_show_ranking_score_injected_unconditionally() {\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n didn't request scores\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n    // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n Adaptive replica selection using EWMA scoring (plan §13.3).\n//!\n//! Replaces round-robin with latency-aware selection using EWMA-smoothed\n//! metrics: latency p95, in-flight request count, and error rate.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse rand::prelude::*;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Replica selection strategy.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum SelectionStrategy {\n    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n    Adaptive,\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    RoundRobin,\n    /// Random selection.\n    Random,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SelectionStrategy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Adaptive\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replica selection configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    /// Selection strategy.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub strategy: String,\n    /// Latency weight in score computation.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_latency_weight\")]\n    pub latency_weight: f64,\n    /// In-flight request weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n    /// Error rate weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n    pub error_weight: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_ewma_half_life\")]\n    pub ewma_half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Exploration epsilon (probability of random selection).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_epsilon\")]\n    pub exploration_epsilon: f64,\n}\n\nfn default_latency_weight() -> f64 {\n    1.0\n}\nfn default_inflight_weight() -> f64 {\n    2.0\n}\nfn default_error_weight() -> f64 {\n    10.0\n}\nfn default_ewma_half_life() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\nfn default_epsilon() -> f64 {\n    0.05\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n            latency_weight: default_latency_weight(),\n            inflight_weight: default_inflight_weight(),\n            error_weight: default_error_weight(),\n            ewma_half_life_ms: default_ewma_half_life(),\n            exploration_epsilon: default_epsilon(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Per-node metrics for adaptive selection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeMetrics {\n    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n    /// Current in-flight request count.\n    pub in_flight: u32,\n    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub error_rate: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Last update timestamp.\n    pub last_updated: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeMetrics {\n    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n            in_flight: 0,\n            error_rate: 0.0,\n            half_life_ms,\n            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment in-flight count.\n    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Callback for reporting selection events.\npub trait SelectionObserver: Send + Sync {\n    /// Called when a node is selected with its score.\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64);\n    /// Called when exploration selects a random node.\n    fn report_exploration(&self);\n}\n\n/// No-op observer for when metrics aren't needed.\nstruct NoOpObserver;\nimpl SelectionObserver for NoOpObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, _node_id: &str, _score: f64) {}\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {}\n}\n\n/// Replica selector.\npub struct ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    /// Per-node metrics.\n    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n    /// Random number generator.\n    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n    /// Observer for selection events.\n    observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>,\n}\n\nimpl ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Create a new replica selector with a metrics observer.\n    pub fn new_with_observer(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig, observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n            observer,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new replica selector without metrics.\n    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::new_with_observer(config, Arc::new(NoOpObserver))\n    }\n\n    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n\n        match strategy {\n            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n\n        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n        if self.should_explore() {\n            self.observer.report_exploration();\n            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n            if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n                let score = metrics\n                    .get(node)\n                    .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                    .unwrap_or(1000.0);\n                self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n            }\n            return selected;\n        }\n\n        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the minimum score\n        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n        let mut best_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = Vec::new();\n\n        for node in candidates {\n            let score = metrics\n                .get(node)\n                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n\n            if score < best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best_nodes.clear();\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            } else if (score - best_score).abs() < 1e-10 {\n                // Scores are essentially equal - add to tie list\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If multiple nodes have the same best score, pick randomly\n        let selected = if best_nodes.len() == 1 {\n            best_nodes.into_iter().next()\n        } else {\n            let idx = self.rng.lock().unwrap().gen_range(0..best_nodes.len());\n            best_nodes.get(idx).cloned()\n        };\n\n        if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n        }\n\n        selected\n    }\n\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Random selection.\n    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n        let idx = self\n            .rng\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n        entry.update_error(false);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed request.\n    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n            entry.update_latency(lat);\n        }\n        entry.update_error(true);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node: &NodeId) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.increment_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Get metrics for a node.\n    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<NodeMetrics> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n        metrics.get(node).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the strategy from config string.\n    fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n            \"random\" => SelectionStrategy::Random,\n            _ => SelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Clone for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: self.config.clone(),\n            metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),\n            rr_counter: Arc::clone(&self.rr_counter),\n            rng: Arc::clone(&self.rng),\n            observer: Arc::clone(&self.observer),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(config.strategy, \"adaptive\");\n        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_score() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(50.0, 5000);\n        assert_eq!(metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default()), 50.0);\n\n        metrics.in_flight = 5;\n        let score = metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n        // 50 * 1.0 + 5 * 2.0 = 60\n        assert_eq!(score, 60.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_ewma() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(100.0, 1000); // Short half-life\n\n        metrics.update_latency(50.0);\n        // Should move toward 50\n        assert!(metrics.latency_p95_ms < 100.0 && metrics.latency_p95_ms > 40.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(true);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate > 0.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        // Error rate should decay\n        let rate_before = metrics.error_rate;\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate < rate_before);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_adaptive() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        // Seed metrics by recording successful requests\n        selector.record_success(&node1, 10.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(&node2, 100.0).await;\n\n        // Should select node-1 (lower score)\n        let candidates = vec![node2.clone(), node1.clone()];\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_round_robin() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n            strategy: \"round_robin\".into(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        let candidates = vec![node1.clone(), node2.clone()];\n\n        // First call should return node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1.clone()));\n\n        // Second call should return node-2\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node2.clone()));\n\n        // Third call should wrap to node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_request_lifecycle() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let node = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n\n        selector.record_request_start(&node).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 1);\n\n        // Record success decrements in-flight and updates latency\n        selector.record_success(&node, 50.0).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        // In-flight should be decremented (from 1 to 0)\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_empty_candidates() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let selected =
